title: pittosporum densiflorum
intro: Pittosporum densiflorum, also known as the 'Kauri tobacco tree', is a plant species native to New Zealand that belongs to the Pittosporaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Pittosporum densiflorum include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Pittosporum densiflorum typically involve infusions or decoctions made from the leaves or roots of the plant, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments.
